Toggle navigation Tetranychus urticae (TETRUR) Menu Overview → BCA Categorization Reporting Photos Documents Overview Code created in: 2002-10-07 Basic information EPPO Code: TETRUR Preferred name: Tetranychus urticae Authority: Koch Notes Studies have shown that Tetranychus urticae and T. cinnabarinnus (carmine spider mite) are synonymous (Auger et al., 2013, Acarologia).       1ANIMK      ) Phylum Arthropoda      (
      1ARTHP      ) Subphylum Chelicerata      (
      1CHELQ      ) Class Arachnida      (
      1ARACC      ) Order Acarida      (
      1ACARO      ) Family Tetranychidae      (
      1TETRF      ) Genus Tetranychus      (
      1TETRG      ) Species Tetranychus urticae      (
      TETRUR      )

seed.symptoms: Fine stippling/bronzing on leaf surface; fine webbing on undersides; leaves turn yellow, dry out, and drop; mites visible under magnification.
seed.causes: Pest Tetranychus urticae (Two-spotted spider mite); thrives in hot, dry conditions; builds resistance quickly to acaricides.
seed.treatment: Acaricides (abamectin, bifenazate); insecticidal soap or neem oil; predatory mites (Phytoseiidae) as biocontrol.
seed.prevention: Maintain plant moisture; avoid dusty conditions; monitor undersides of leaves early; avoid broad-spectrum insecticides that kill natural predators.
